seoTitle: Connect Facebook Leads to HubSpot Easily
metaDescription: Sync Facebook Lead Ads with HubSpot CRM by confirming Meta permissions, authorizing OAuth access, and mapping form fields to contact properties.
intro: Connecting Facebook Lead Ads to HubSpot means new leads flow into your CRM automatically instead of getting exported manually from Meta's ad manager. This guide covers verifying your Meta Business Suite permissions, authorizing the connection, and mapping your form fields to matching CRM properties. It closes with running a test to confirm ingestion is active.

steps.heading: Check your Meta permissions
steps.body: Confirm you have administrative, page, and ad account access inside Meta Business Suite.

steps.heading: Open Ads settings in HubSpot
steps.body: Go to Settings, then Marketing, and select the Ads configuration panel.

steps.heading: Connect your Facebook account
steps.body: Click Connect Account and select Facebook from the available network options.

steps.heading: Pass the OAuth gateway
steps.body: Complete the Meta OAuth screen, review permissions, and confirm the account link.

steps.heading: Choose your lead forms
steps.body: Switch to Lead Syncing to view connected pages and select active lead capture forms, enabling the marketing contacts toggle.

steps.heading: Map fields and test
steps.body: Match custom form questions to CRM parameters, then trigger a test run to confirm the sync.

faq.question: What permissions do I need in Meta to connect Facebook leads?
faq.answer: You need administrative, page, and ad account permissions inside Meta Business Suite before connecting.

faq.question: Does connecting Facebook leads require OAuth authentication?
faq.answer: Yes, you'll pass through Meta's OAuth handshake to grant HubSpot access to your lead data.

faq.question: Can I choose which Facebook forms sync to HubSpot?
faq.answer: Yes, the Lead Syncing tab lets you select specific business pages and active lead capture forms.

faq.question: How do I verify the lead sync is working?
faq.answer: You can trigger a test run through the Meta Lead Ads testing component after saving your configuration.
